title: Episode Titles
wikitext:
[]: Episode Titles             Quote Post by CyberXIII » Thu Apr 30, 2009 4:55 pm What did the titles of the different episodes mean? Some of them were obvious (Both of you, Dance like you want to win!), but some just have me confused (Ambivalence, Splitting of the breast). Were the supposed to have some connection to the episode, or what?

[]:             Quote Post by Xeroko » Thu Apr 30, 2009 9:14 pm For Ambivalence, I suppose it could refer to the conflicting emotions Shinji feels when he has to fight Bardiel. He wants to pilot so he can have an identity yet at the same time he doesn't want to hurt his friend. Either that or perhaps how how he feels about his father. He seemed happy when he was praised for defeating Sahaquiel and then he is forced by the same man to nearly kill his own friend.

[]:             Quote Post by Joseph the PRPD » Fri May 01, 2009 12:08 pm Episode 26(TV) should have been titled "Congradulations" and episode 26(EoE) should have been titled "We're all fucked now"

[]:             Quote Post by thewayneiac » Fri May 01, 2009 7:15 pm Joseph the PRPD wrote: Episode 26(TV) should have been titled "Congradulations" and episode 26(EoE) should have been titled "We're all fucked now"

[]:             Quote Post by Oedipusfoot » Fri May 01, 2009 7:33 pm I know there's a definite definition of "Splitting of the Breast" but I don't remember it. When I watch that episode I always think about Shinji's growing awareness/dependence from his mother.

[]:             Quote Post by NAveryW » Fri May 01, 2009 7:57 pm The titles are all sexual innuendo. "Oral Stage", for example, is the Freudian stage of psychosexual development in which sexual pleasure is gained through the mouth. It's a superseded theory, of course, but NGE doesn't care.

[]:             Quote Post by ZapX » Fri May 01, 2009 8:01 pm " Sickness unto Death " is a book by Søren Kierkegaard. A classic existentialist text.

[]:             Quote Post by FallenTabris » Sat May 02, 2009 5:35 am thewayneiac wrote: Joseph the PRPD wrote: Episode 26(TV) should have been titled "Congradulations" and episode 26(EoE) should have been titled "We're all fucked now"

[]:             Quote Post by Alaska Slim » Sat May 02, 2009 7:02 am NAveryW wrote: The titles are all sexual innuendo.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Sat May 02, 2009 10:02 am Alaska Slim wrote: Well, the crazy bastards on this forum seem to think it should be translated as "Sincerely Yours", due to it aligning with a sentiment the Japanese use to end their letters, but, whose to say?

[]:             Quote Post by Shin-seiki » Sat May 02, 2009 11:32 am The Music Man wrote: How can there be any sin in sincere? Where is the good in goodbye? Your apprehensions confuse me dear Puzzle and mystify (Mystify) Tell me what can be fair in farewell, dear While one single star shines above How can there be any sin in sincere? Aren't we sincerely in love? Oh, we're in love!

[]:             Quote Post by Alaska Slim » Sat May 02, 2009 1:08 pm Reichu wrote: Difference between a 'literal translation' and a fluent translation....

[]:             Quote Post by TheLobe » Sun May 03, 2009 1:01 am I always thought eva titles were written as twos because they thought of two awesome phrases and didn't know which one to use.

[]:             Quote Post by CyberXIII » Thu May 07, 2009 9:01 pm "She said, "Don't make others suffer for your personal hatred.""

[]:             Quote Post by LeoXiao » Thu May 07, 2009 10:04 pm Regarding the episode called "Air," I was watching the preview for it (at the end of episode 24, the one where Kaoru dies), and I noticed that it had a bunch of scenes from EoE. So was Anno planning on making EoE from the beginning anyway?

[]:             Quote Post by Alaska Slim » Fri May 08, 2009 12:30 am TheLobe wrote: I always thought eva titles were written as twos because they thought of two awesome phrases and didn't know which one to use.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Fri May 08, 2009 5:58 am LeoXiao wrote: Regarding the episode called "Air," I was watching the preview for it (at the end of episode 24, the one where Kaoru dies), and I noticed that it had a bunch of scenes from EoE.

[]:             Quote Post by TheLobe » Sat May 09, 2009 11:18 pm Those women longed for the touch of others' lips, and thus invited their kisses. I remember this title because it seemed so clunky. By touch of others does it refer to each other, or people other than themselves. The episode has kisses between Shinji and Asuka and Kaji and Misato. Also develops the Rei Yui similarity, so i assume thats what the titles about.

[]:             Quote Post by Alaska Slim » Sat May 09, 2009 11:30 pm Reichu wrote: Alaska Slim wrote: Yes he did, as the announcement came only two weeks afterwards.

[]: Nietzche episode title             Quote Post by schismatics » Mon May 11, 2009 1:40 pm ok isn't "The Beast that Shouted I at the Heart of the World" a quote from Nietzche?? I just read "The Birth of Tragedy" like a week or two ago and I stumbled upon a quote that was similar to TBTSIATHOTW (by the way I DO NOT recommend reading The Birth of Tragedy). I was just wondering if anyone knew any more information on the subject.

Some of them were obvious (Both of you, Dance like you want to win!), but some just have me confused (Ambivalence, Splitting of the breast). Were the supposed to have some connection to the episode, or what?

[]:             Quote Post by Xerok
